route.name: Central Wasatch Traverse
route.description: Public GPX mountain traverse with singletrack/trail sections, forest roads, paved connectors, and high-elevation logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX mountain traverse with singletrack/trail sections, forest roads, paved connectors, and high-elevation logistics.
route.bestSeason: Summer/fall after snow clears; July needs storms, trail rules, and camp logistics checked.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX mountain traverse with singletrack/trail sections, forest roads, paved connectors, and high-elevation logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Mountain water likely exists but spacing and treatment need GPX-based measurement.
resources.food: Wasatch service access varies by segment; verify stops and hours.
resources.camping: Camp/lodging sequence must be chosen and verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until trail legality and camp sequence are checked.
Use wider tires and early starts.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject_rework; public GPX exists, but the July 10-13 window, legal overnights, watershed restrictions, closure overlays, and water/forecast refreshes are not solved
blockers: Not ride-ready until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
The public route is effectively five riding days in the guide; a July 10-13 version is not proven.
No exact legal overnight sites or exact-date lodging bookings are confirmed for July 10, July 11, and July 12.
Stealth camping and lake/trail-adjacent camping cannot satisfy the legal overnight requirement.
Cottonwood watershed rules, American Fork camping restrictions, Mill Creek closure, and Mount Timpanogos Wilderness boundary need spatial proof against the GPX.
High Wasatch thunderstorm, smoke, fire, and water-source conditions cannot be cleared from July 2 for July 10-13.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend ride readiness.
Rebuild as either a verified five-day itinerary or a hard four-day itinerary with exact daily endpoints.
Replace every stealth/dispersed overnight with confirmed legal coordinates, developed campground reservations, or exact-date lodging bookings.
Overlay the GPX against Cottonwood watershed rules, American Fork camping restrictions, Mill Creek closure map, and Mount Timpanogos Wilderness.
Verify bike legality for each named singletrack segment and intended direction.
Refresh water sources, Uinta-Wasatch-Cache alerts, Utah Fire Info, AirNow, NWS forecasts, and thunderstorm/fire-weather outlooks on July 8-10.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: High Wasatch route may be in season, but storms, snow patches, trail rules, and camp logistics must be checked.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.finalDecisionNote: Source-backed route remains planning-grade; target-window blockers prevent ride-ready promotion only.
